title: 'Rohit Sharma will continue to represent India; Lord's ODI won't be his last': BCCI
content: BCCI secretary Devajit Saikia has dismissed speculation about Rohit Sharma's ODI retirement. He confirmed that Rohit remains a regular member of India's ODI team. Rohit will continue to represent the country as long as he is in the scheme of things. The selectors are assessing all players for the 2027 World Cup preparations. Therefore, the Lord's ODI will not be Rohit's final match.

title: Trump threatens new tariffs on Canada over wildfire smoke affecting US cities
content: President Trump threatened new tariffs on Canada due to wildfire smoke.  He accused Ottawa of failing to manage forests and causing unhealthy air.  Smoke from hundreds of Canadian wildfires has spread across many United States states.  Republican lawmakers also criticized Canada's response to the recurring smoke.  Canadian officials have not yet responded to the tariff warning.

title: Scientists fit 75 pronghorns, fastest land animal in Americas, with GPS collars: What they found
content: GPS collars track pronghorn movement near a New Mexico solar project.  Fences around the solar farm present a significant barrier to these animals.  Pronghorn typically crawl under fences rather than jumping over them.  Researchers suggest simple fence modifications could aid wildlife movement.  These changes would help animals use their traditional migration paths.

title: Winners will make the hard choices: Microsoft's security chief to employees after layoffs
content: Microsoft's new security chief Hayete Gallot has replaced senior executives, merged engineering teams, and cut hundreds of jobs to push the company toward AI-powered cybersecurity, The Information reports. In an internal memo, Gallot told staff the industry is being reimagined and rewards firms that make hard choices early. Microsoft is chasing AI security spending flowing to rivals OpenAI and Anthropic.

title: Get paid 88,000 yen to spend a night proving a haunted house is ghost-free
content: Japan's unusual "ghost investigation" job is attracting attention as specialists are being hired to spend an entire night inside so-called haunted houses to prove they are free of paranormal activity. The service costs 88,000 yen (around Rs 31,000 or $542) per property and is helping owners sell or rent homes that have been abandoned because of their tragic past.

title: Sarah Baartman, 'Hottentot Venus', finally came home 192 years later
content: Sarah Baartman's life was marked by colonial violence and exploitation in South Africa.  She was exhibited in Europe as the "Hottentot Venus" for voyeuristic curiosity.  French scientists studied her body after her death, which fueled racial science narratives.  Her remains were eventually repatriated to South Africa after nearly two centuries.  Her story remains a powerful emblem of remembrance and justice.

title: US may add $100,000 bond to Green Card applicants seeking permanent residence
content: The US government is considering a $100,000 bond for Green Card applicants. This measure aims to ensure immigrants are financially self-sufficient and do not burden public programs. The Trump administration is exploring this long-standing legal authority under the Immigration and Nationality Act. This proposal follows earlier attempts to impose fees on H-1B visa applications. Officials stated this policy reduced overstays in pilot programs.

title: Delhi high court order has crippled consular, visa services at 4 missions: Govt to Supreme Court
content: Visa and consular services are severely impacted in four Indian missions abroad. The Delhi High Court had quashed the selection of private service providers for these crucial functions. The Centre informed the Supreme Court that these services are now at a standstill. This cancellation has forced missions to divert staff to meet emergency needs. The Supreme Court agreed to hear the Centre's appeal on Monday.

title: Who is Tiana Musarra? Meet the influencer making waves at Streamer University 2026
content: Tiana Musarra is a lifestyle influencer, model and Twitch streamer who gained wider recognition after joining Kai Cenat’s Streamer University 2026. She previously built an audience through fashion, fitness and GRWM content and has over 123,000 Twitch followers. Her name recently drew extra attention after Josh “YourRAGE” mentioned her during a heated on-stream call with DDG.

title: Delimitation debate: How opposition faultlines may help govt in Monsoon session
content: There are strong speculations that the government is planning to reintroduce the Delimitation Bill during upcoming Monsoon Session. The Bill could not pass the numbers test during the Budget session. Opposition parties, which were united in their opposition to the Bill in April, appear to be divided now over the idea of uniform seat increase.  Some parties may support the bill if their concerns are addressed.  The Congress fears isolation and has requested an all-party meeting for discussions.  The proposed 50% seat increase aims to facilitate women's reservation without altering current proportions.

title: Nolan Wells update: Eerie Horn Island discovery detail raises fresh questions
content: Rescuer Brian Trascher says the strong currents and sudden underwater drop-off at Horn Island’s northwest tip may explain how Nolan Wells died. Authorities are still waiting for autopsy results and say they have found no evidence of foul play. Meanwhile, Nolan’s family is pursuing an independent investigation, and the case will also be presented to a grand jury.

title: Why Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are delaying their honeymoon despite recent wedding
content: Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift have spent their first weeks of married life moving between Montana, California, and Florida rather than on a traditional honeymoon. The couple attended a friend's wedding, trained, and stayed largely out of public view. Sources say Travis's July 28 Chiefs training camp deadline is the primary factor shaping whatever private trip they may still plan. Insiders report the couple has kept the destination secret even from close family, with plans to share details only once they are on the way back.

title: Blake Lively left heartbroken as Gigi Hadid reportedly deepens Taylor Swift rift
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce kept the drama away from their New York City wedding as they invited pretty much everyone from Hollywood and the NFL but Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds, sparking chaos among many. While this has sparked a mixed reaction among fans, it seems supermodel Gigi Hadid might have made things worse for Blake Lively.

title: Who is Harper Seven Beckham? Meet David and Victoria Beckham's only daughter
content: Harper Seven Beckham is now one of the most celebrity offspring in the world. She is the only daughter of soccer icon David Beckham and fashion designer Victoria Beckham and has lived her life in the public eye while keeping a relatively private existence. From her famous name to her fashion appearances, here’s everything you need to know about Harper Seven Beckham

title: 1 in 4 Kerala candidates skips NEET, Rajasthan posts 69% success
content: NEET-UG 2026 attendance revealed regional disparities across India. Kerala, Tamil Nadu, and Karnataka experienced significant candidate absenteeism this year. Rajasthan, however, saw increased registrations and a strong qualification rate. Other northern states like Uttar Pradesh also reported lower absentee numbers. This shift highlights evolving patterns in medical entrance examination participation.

title: UK court denies bail to Indian captain of sanctioned Russian oil vessel
content: An Indian captain faces sanctions violation charges after his oil tanker was seized. He was denied bail by a London court due to flight risk concerns. A four-week trial is scheduled to begin in mid-December. The captain is accused of transporting sanctioned Russian oil products. Twenty-four crew members remain aboard the detained vessel.

title: Build toilets for women in courts in 6 weeks: Supreme Court
content: The Supreme Court has mandated states to construct washrooms for women lawyers. This directive addresses a significant human rights and health issue for advocates. States are given a six-week deadline to complete the construction of these facilities. The court rejected pleas of fund paucity, suggesting alternative revenue sources. This action aims to ensure women lawyers can practice with dignity and health.

title: Unlike last time, DMK is clear it won’t follow Congress
content: Political momentum builds for the government's Constitution amendment bill.  DMK and NCP (SP) signals suggest support for the upcoming legislation.  This comes after recent political realignments and party splits.  The government plans to incorporate changes to assure fence-sitters.  The bill aims to increase Lok Sabha seats and implement women's reservation.

title: KBC 18 format tweaks: Promos pitch thinking over rote answers; AI era sets the tone
content: Amitabh Bachchan will host the eighteenth season of Kaun Banega Crorepati starting August 10. New promos highlight the show's updated focus on critical thinking and decision-making skills. Memorizing answers will no longer suffice for contestants aiming for the grand prize. The show acknowledges the impact of artificial intelligence and the need for evolving knowledge application.

title: Dept of Space vacancy rate at 25-year high; nearly 3 in 10 posts lie vacant
content: India's space department faces a growing manpower crunch, with over twenty-five percent of sanctioned posts vacant. This staffing gap has widened significantly since 2019, impacting critical mission development. Recruitment processes were delayed due to Covid-19 and reforms, creating a substantial gap. Efforts are underway to fill over two thousand vacancies by late 2026. The department must rebuild its workforce while retaining experienced scientists.

title: Baptism of Yamal by 'Saint' Messi: The photo that became WC final's origin story
content: Made in La Masia:  Lamine Yamal vs Lionel Messi. How a photograph captures the unlikely meeting of two of Barcelona's most famous graduates. Read more in today's Offside.

title: Congress to join NC’s Jantar Mantar protest on Monday, BJP plans secretariat gherao
content: Jammu and Kashmir politics intensifies as the National Conference plans a protest in New Delhi. The Congress party announced its participation in the proposed sit-in demonstration. Meanwhile, the BJP has scheduled a protest against the current government's alleged corruption. Peoples Conference chief Sajad Lone questioned the timing and motives behind the protest. He suggested an assembly session and an all-party delegation instead of street protests.

title: 'A true icon of the game': BCCI pays tribute to Garry Sobers with throwback video
content: The BCCI honoured Sir Garfield Sobers, a true cricket icon, after his passing. He was remembered as one of the game's greatest all-rounders ever. Sobers left an enduring legacy which will continue to inspire many. Cricket West Indies also posted a heartfelt farewell to the legend. His remarkable career redefined greatness in the sport.

title: Net shut, Doda on edge after man shot dead by cops in cattle smuggling stakeout
content: A man was shot dead in Doda district following an encounter with police personnel.  Mobile internet services were suspended across the district as tensions escalated rapidly.  Three police officers sustained injuries during the confrontation on Jai valley road.  Locals protested the death, and a shutdown was called by a local outfit.  Authorities are investigating the incident, and senior officials are present in Bhaderwah.

title: Tommy Paul marries longtime girlfriend Paige Lorenze in stunning Long Island wedding
content: Paige Lorenze and Tommy Paul married on July 14 at Old Westbury Gardens in New York after getting engaged in July 2025. Paige revealed the news by changing her Instagram name before sharing wedding photos with Vogue. The couple celebrated with family and friends, choosing a venue that reflected where their relationship first began.

title: Horoscope Today: Daily astrological predictions for July 18, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for July 18, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: Techie hangs self; family takes body to boyfriend's village, seeks posthumous wedding
content: A 24-year-old software engineer from Andhra Pradesh allegedly died by suicide in her Mumbai PG after her boyfriend reportedly refused to marry her. Her family took her body to his village in Prakasam district, demanding he fulfil her alleged last wish by tying a mangalsutra before cremation. The protest triggered tension before police intervened and the last rites were conducted.

title: Delhi cab driver dies after iron rod from overloaded e-rickshaw tears through windshield
content: A cab driver was killed and a woman passenger seriously injured after an iron rod protruding from an e-rickshaw pierced the cab’s windshield and struck the driver during a collision on the Dwarka Expressway in Gurugram on Friday. Police suspect overspeeding and are examining CCTV footage to determine the sequence of events. The e-rickshaw driver has been detained and an investigation is underway.

title: SLC breaks silence after Manjot Kalra's arrest, vows zero tolerance for corruption
content: Sri Lanka Cricket reaffirmed its zero-tolerance policy on corruption following an arrest. Former India U-19 star Manjot Kalra faces allegations of corrupt practice during the LPL. The ongoing investigation will not impact the tournament's scheduled continuation. Sri Lanka Cricket pledged full cooperation with the police special investigation unit. Robust anti-corruption measures remain in place to protect the league's integrity.

title: Three men sentenced to jail in separate cases of raping, abducting, and harassing minor girl
content: In a series of decisive judgments, a special court in Kalaburagi handed a 25-year jail term to a prime accused for rape, while sentencing two other youths to three years of imprisonment each for abducting and sexually harassing the same 12-year-old survivor.

title: Rajasthan woman Sarpanch highlights rural innovations at BRICS Ministerial Meeting
content: Sarpanch Neeru Yadav represented India at the global event under India’s presidency as a grassroots woman representative and panelist

title: Babulal Marandi writes to Hemant Soren demanding a judicial probe into the alleged scam in JSDMS
content: He also demanded that a special audit of all financial and administrative decisions taken by the JSDMS from the year 2023-24 to the present should be conducted by the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India or an independent agency.

title: Jantar Mantar protest updates: Opposition support grows as Wangchuk’s hunger strike continues on day 20
content: “The medical team stated that Sonam Wangchuk continues to be under 24-hour medical vigilance, with his health being monitored closely,” the CJP said

title: Congress alleges irregularities in gram sabha approvals for Ken-Betwa project
content: Leader of the Opposition in the State Assembly Umang Singhar alleged corruption worth crores in the distribution of compensation and in awarding contracts to companies.

title: Congress moves no-confidence motion against Sai government in Chhattisgarh Assembly
content: During the debate, the Opposition targeted the government with a 136-point chargesheet covering a wide range of issues, including mining, law and order, and tribal rights

title: Groping breasts not part of routine gynaecological examination, says Gauhati High Court
content: Considering the convicted doctor’s age, the court reduces his sentence in the 17-year-old case to ₹5,000 in default of imprisonment for two months

title: ‘Sharad Pawar should take the final call on allying with NDA’
content: Merger unlikely, say leaders from NCP and NCP(SP); Eknath Shinde stays out, as anxiety in NCP(SP) camp grows

title: In Gurugram, 60 rounds, 120 seconds
content: On July 9, a gunfight in Gurugram’s upscale Sushant Lok left four alleged shooters dead and three policemen injured. Police said gangster Deepak Nandal ordered the attack on businessman Vishal Berry over a ₹5 crore extortion demand. Among the dead were two minors from Rohtak’s Bhalaut village with no criminal record. Ashok Kumar finds that the incident has raised questions over crime, drug use, and policing

title: Kafir screenshot case in Kerala: Court rejects SIT’s plea to cancel bail of prime accused Jithin Bhaskaran
content: The prosecution’s argument is that, after being released on bail on July 3, Jithin Bhaskaran violated bail conditions by posting messages on Facebook that allegedly challenged the investigating team

title: Madras High Court refuses to quash FIRs booked against political commentator Ponraj for remarks against TVK women supporters
content: The petitioner, being a person of considerable standing in society, ought to have exercised greater restraint while making public statements, says Justice G.K. Ilanthiraiyan

title: Freedom of press and accountability are important: Delhi High Court
content: Justice Girish Kathpalia made the observation on July 16, while granting bail to two persons allegedly involved in assaulting two reporters freelancing for a YouTube channel

title: India’s gig internet workforce may reach 17-21 million by 2030, says Bengaluru firm Redseer
content: Across delivery, ride-hailing and home services, full-time gig workers earn, on average, up to 2.5 times the monthly net income of workers in comparable formal and informal occupations

title: India rejects former Japan Minister’s remarks on Shinkansen project delay
content: The clarification comes in response to a recent critical social media post by a former Japanese Minister regarding the project, which will be India’s first bullet train network.

title: Oceansat-3A likely to be launched by year-end, says NRSC Director
content: Coastal observation research laboratory inaugurated at Andhra University’s Centre for Studies on Bay of Bengal

title: Supreme Court opposes use of SIR data for non-poll tasks
content: The top court issues notice to the Election Commission and Bengal government after Congress leader’s petition says the State is denying welfare benefits to people excluded from electoral rolls

title: Special team to conduct detailed probe into Dharani transactions
content: The government had already ordered a audit which probed transactions in Siddipet and Medak districts on a pilot basis, and the exercise was extended to the remaining parts of State; Cabinet takes the decision following spate of complaints like inclusion of vast lands in prohibited list

title: Investors are returning, says Suvendu; lays foundation stone for ₹15,000-crore steel plant in Bankura
content: Chief Minister promises investor-friendly climate with adequate facilities and conducive law-and-order situation

title: SC extends Bengaluru civic polls to December due to Karnataka SIR
content: The plea had cited “severe logistical challenges” due to the ongoing Special Intensive Revision (SIR) in Karnataka

title: Railways contributed immensely to nation’s overall development: Gehlot
content: Governor of Karnataka Thawar Chand Gehlot has said that the Indian Railways had not only strengthened the Indian economy but also had contributed immensely to the overall development of the country.

title: Prime Minister virtually inaugurates redeveloped Koppal railway station under Amrit Station Scheme
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi virtually inaugurated the redeveloped Koppal station under the Amrit Bharat Station Scheme

title: Another case of minor girl getting pregnant comes to light in Dharwad
content: Close on the heels of a minor girl delivering a baby, another case of minor girl getting pregnant because of alleged sexual exploitation has come to light in Dharwad district.

title: Sajad Lone, Altaf Bukhari will not join NC’s Statehood protest in Delhi; Congress to take part
content: While Sajad Lone questioned both the timing and real motive, J&K Apni Party chief Altaf Bukhari said the NC’s proposed Jantar Mantar protest “is to empower its government not to empower people”

title: India’s electoral roll is a living document: CEC
content: The highest-ever poll participation in the recent Assembly elections is a proof of the trust the electors have in the country’s electoral system and the vibrancy of the  democracy, says Chief Election Commissioner

title: Opposition support grows as Wangchuk’s hunger strike continues on day 20
content: The protest seeking Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation attracted more high-profile visitors

title: Mandatory training for staff to make hospitals patient-friendly
content: Decision to make soft skills training mandatory for hospital employees was taken in  the backdrop of innumerable  complaints from patients and visitors regarding poor behaviour by hospital staff, communication lapses, and unsatisfactory interactions with the public, says Health Minister

title: Residents of a local body cannot claim burial or cremation rights in another local body: HC
content: However, such a right is enforceable if it partakes the character of a fundamental right under Article 25 of the Constitution or a customary right, said a Division Bench of Justices G.R. Swaminathan and K.K. Ramakrishnan

title: HC grants interim bail to Sub-Registrar Justin Manikandan Subramanian in the Palani math fraudulent land registration case
content: Justice K. Rajasekar directed the petitioner to report before the CB-CID at 10.30 a.m. every day from July 27 till August 3, except on Sunday, and co-operate in the investigation.  The larger conspiracy behind the registration of the document was still under investigation and the petitioner was involved in two previous criminal cases of a similar nature, says the State government

title: One more U.S. made anti-submarine helicopter delivered to the Indian Navy
content: The MH-60R is among the world’s most advanced shipborne helicopters; it is designed primarily for anti-submarine and anti-surface warfare

title: Fearing fake certificates, Centre orders stricter scrutiny of digitised birth and death records
content: Complaints regarding issuance of fake/unauthorised digitised certificates in some instances not only undermines the integrity of the Civil Registration System but also threatens the reliability of vital records, says Deputy Registrar General in letter to States

title: Leather Jacket Worn By Nvidia CEO Auctions For Nearly $1 Million
content: A black leather jacket sported by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang sold for $960,000 at a New York auction on Friday, 16 times higher than the estimated value of $60,000, according to Sotheby's.

title: US Hits Iran 7th Night In Row, Tehran Replies With "No Border Safe" Warning
content: US forces launched strikes against Iran for a seventh night in a row on Friday, the US military said.

title: Elon Musk's SpaceX In Talks To Provide Computing Power To Pentagon: Report
content: Elon Musk's SpaceX is in talks to provide the US Department of Defense with access to data center capacity worth billions of dollars to run AI models, the Wall Street Journal reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

title: Smoke, Fire, Floods: US Faces Triple Extreme Summer Weather Threats
content: Wildfire smoke blanketed the eastern US from the Great Lakes to Washington, DC on Friday, floodwaters tore through Texas's Hill Country for a third day, and new fires erupted in the Pacific Northwest overnight, with 68 large blazes now burning in 15 states.

title: Venezuela Earthquake: Death Count Rises To More Than 5,000
content: The twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela last month killed over 5,000 people, National Assembly chief Jorge Rodriguez posted on Telegram Friday.

title: 'No Border Will Be Safe': Iran Warns Of Full-Scale Offensive Against US
content: Tehran is ready to resume "full-scale offensive operations" if US strikes against it continue for another two or three days, a senior military advisor to Iran's supreme leader warned Friday.

title: US-Iran War News LIVE: US Launches Fresh Strikes Against Iran As Hormuz Tensions Intensify
content: US-Iran War News Live Updates: Iran claimed it destroyed "several US refuelling aircraft and fighter jets" and caused "serious damage to many more".

title: Human Face Of Noida Tragedy: She Prayed Through Cab Ride. He Was Already Gone
content: On an ordinary Wednesday, a phone call changed everything for two sisters in Delhi. NDTV spoke to them about what happens the moment someone you love becomes one of the victims of a fire.

title: Weather Office Warns Of Heavy Rain In Northeast Over Next 4 Days
content: Within Arunachal Pradesh, the Meteorological Centre at Itanagar has identified Papum Pare and Kurung Kumey as the districts facing the highest risk during the current spell.

title: Court Adjourns Hearing In Krishna Janmabhumi-Shahi Idgah Mosque Dispute
content: The Allahabad High Court on Friday adjourned the hearing in the Mathura Krishna Janmabhumi-Shahi Idgah mosque dispute matter till August 25.

title: "We Don't Want Them": Trump Set To Replace Immigrant Truckers With Veterans
content: Addressing the military investment summit, Trump said his administration was preparing to take what he called "historic action" against undocumented truck drivers.

title: UK Nationalises British Steel, Takes Control From China's Jingye
content: The country's last factory that can make steel from scratch faced imminent closure last year after Jingye said the plant in Scunthorpe, northern England, was no longer financially viable.

title: "Trying To Find New Purpose": Parents Of Boy Killed In Mumbai Tree Collapse
content: Speaking to NDTV's Shiv Aroor on Friday, Gaurav and Juhi Srivastav described how their daily existence had revolved around their only son.

title: PM Modi's "Vande Mataram" Message Set To Fly Into Space Today
content: As India's private space sector prepares for another defining moment, a special message from Prime Minister Narendra Modi is set to ride into space aboard Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 rocket.

title: Rs 2,000-Crore Delhi Secretariat Plan Likely To Get Global Bids Next Month
content: The proposed Rs 2,000-crore twin-tower complex at ITO is planned as the Delhi government's new headquarters, bringing departments operating from several locations across the capital under one roof.

title: Another MH-60R Seahawk Helicopter Delivered To Navy: US Embassy
content: The US Embassy on Friday said that another MH-60R Seahawk helicopter has been delivered to the Indian Navy and two more are arriving soon, underlining that the bilateral defence partnership between the countries is "growing stronger".

title: Secret Service 'Fed Up' With Vance Family's Last-Minute Travel Demands: Report
content: One incident that particularly angered agents involved a request to fly JD Vance and his young son across Washington aboard Marine Two for a golf lesson.

title: Meta Can Fire Staff Who Sued It For AI Discrimination, US Judge Rules
content: US District Judge William Orrick in Oakland, California, in a written order said he would not stop Meta from carrying out the layoffs beginning July 22 while the merits of the workers' novel legal claims are decided in private arbitration.

title: NCP Merger First, NDA Entry Later: BJP's Big Maharashtra Plan
content: This development comes amid rumblings of political reunions in Maharashtra, fuelled by recent meetings between leaders from both NCP camps and key figures in the ruling dispensation.

title: Video: Shashi Tharoor Stuck In Kerala Hotel Lift, Smiles After Being Rescued
content: The incident happened at a private hotel when the Congress MP was arriving to attend the installation ceremony of the Rotary Club of Trivandrum East.

title: India to engage with US over immigration & visa issues
content:
Representative image

NEW DELHI: Ministry of external affairs said on Friday that while visa rules and immigration matters are sovereign decisions of any country, India will continue to engage with the US on the difficulties faced by Indian travellers and students seeking to visit the US. "When issues concerning genuine travellers seeking to visit the US are brought to our attention, we take them up with the US side to minimise difficulties for our people," MEA spokesperson said. He was responding to queries related to the US's tightening of visa rules for foreign students, exchange visitors and journalists. The new regulations establish a fixed period of admission for non-immigrant visa holders in F, J, and I classifications, Department of Homeland Security said in a statement on Thursday. The F category visas are for students, the J category for exchange visitors, and the I category is for journalists.

End of Article

content: three activists on indefinite hunger strike at Jantar Mantar have seen their health deteriorate further on the 20th day of their protest . doctors advised immediate hospitalisation for one of them due to hypoglycaemia . aameen has developed very high uric acid levels and has lost nearly 14 per cent of his body weight .

title: India’s first, 2 geothermal wells commissioned in Ladakh
content: ONGC Energy Centre would execute two geothermal wells in the Union Territory . the two 1,000m-deep wells, at an altitude of over 14,000 ft, would significantly advance ladakh’s journey towards becoming a clean energy hub .

title: Watch: Deadly landslide strikes China, dozens remain missing
content: the landslide struck at 9.08 am in Pengshui Miao and Tujia Autonomous County . despite the early warning, huge volumes of rock and mud crashed down the mountainside . authorities said 18 people had been rescued, including two with serious injuries .

title: Bridges, airport, railway station: US attacks civilian infrastructure in Iran
content: u.s. intensifies its renewed bombing campaign against Iran . at least five bridges in southern Iran were hit in the strikes . seven people were reported killed in attacks on bridges . an airport in Iranshahr, near the border with Pakistan, was also struck .

title: 'Gandhi' film not on Mahatma’: Aamir faces political backlash for remarks on Sonam
content: aamir Khan's remarks on Sonam Wangchuk sparks political backlash . he says neither he nor director knew him when they made '3 idiots' . political parties accuse him of being unwilling to take a stand out of fear of the ruling establishment .

title: 4 schoolkids die as train rams pool car at open level crossing, gateman arrested
content: a passenger train rammed a pool car ferrying seven students at the gate in Murshidabad . a 65-year-old cyclist died on being knocked down by the train . three other students and the driver of the pool car — a Tata Sumo — were hospitalised .

title: No doctor would refuse treatment to brutally sexually assaulted child: Supreme Court
content: a doctor, accused of refusing admission to the child taken to a private hospital in the city, says he was only a BAMS doctor . a bench of the justices says no doctor worth his salt would refuse treatment . "if you cannot give medical assistance, do not prefix 'Dr' before your name," the bench says .

title: Girls have higher success rate but boys lead 690+ NEET club
content: women accounted for 13.3 lakh of 22.8 lakh registrations, or 58.5%, and 6.5 lakh of 11.2 lakh candidates who qualified in the re-exam . women made up only 21.7% of the 690-plus list, underlining a persistent pipeline-to-pinnacle gap .

title: DMK open to talks on delimitation bill 'if Tamil Nadu interests not hurt'
content: DMK says it is open to negotiation if govt can guarantee that the interests of Tamil Nadu and other southern states will not be compromised due to delimitation . "we had conveyed even last time that we can reconsider if there is an ironclad guarantee there will be no discrimination," the functionary says .

title: Supreme Court to take up high cost of lifesaver meds

title: Bullet train on track, Japan ex-min's comments 'factually incorrect': Govt
content: former justice minister hideki Makihara accuses india of not keeping their promises . he blames india for lack of progress in work on the 'bullet train' project . the comments follow the india-japan summit that saw PM Sanae Takaichi and PM Narendra Modi reaffirming the importance of the high-speed rail as a flagship project between the two countries .

title: EC can delete doubtful voters, cannot decide citizenship: Supreme Court
content: a bench of justices says EC has the constitutional power to delete a person's name from the voter list if his/her citizenship was doubtful . only 1% of SIR appeals decided, welfare benefits denied, SC says . the bench says the state govt was denying benefits of social welfare schemes .
